title: CPQ Exercises — Foundation
description: Hands-on exercises covering CPQ fundamentals: navigation, users, companies, product catalog setup, and guided selling.

sections.title: Exercise 1 — Company, User Type & User Setup
sections.content:
Objective

Set up the foundational user structure in your CPQ sandbox tenant — a company, a user type, and a user — and understand how these objects relate to each other.

Steps

Part A — Create a Company

  1. Navigate to Setup → Users → Companies.
  2. Click Add New Company.
  3. Enter a name using the pattern YourInitials_TechCorp (e.g. JD_TechCorp).
  4. Set the Company Code to TC01.
  5. Fill in address and contact details with realistic dummy data.
  6. Save the company.

Part B — Create a User Type

  1. Navigate to Setup → Users → User Types.
  2. Create a user type named YourInitials_SalesRep.
  3. Enable Show Prices and Allow Adding Incomplete Items.
  4. Save.
  5. Create a second user type named YourInitials_SalesManager with the same settings.

Part C — Create a User

  1. Navigate to Setup → Users.
  2. Create a new user with your own name and email.
  3. Set the User Type to YourInitials_SalesRep and the Company to YourInitials_TechCorp.
  4. Set date format, number format, and time zone to your locale.
  5. Save and verify you can describe the relationship: 1 user → 1 company, 1 user → 1 user type.

Validation

Log out and log back in with your new user. Confirm that the catalog is visible and that your company name appears in the user context. Try switching to the SalesManager user type and note what changes in the UI.

sections.title: Exercise 2 — Build a Product Catalog from Scratch
sections.content:
Objective

Create a complete product catalog structure with categories, subcategories, simple products, and a configurable product — following the required top-down creation order.

Steps

Part A — Categories

  1. Navigate to Setup → Product Catalog → Categories.
  2. Create three top-level categories: YourInitials_Networking (Rank 10), YourInitials_Storage (Rank 20), YourInitials_Accessories (Rank 30). Set all to Active and restrict permissions to your company.
  3. Under Networking, create two subcategories: YourInitials_Routers (Rank 12) and YourInitials_Switches (Rank 14).

Part B — Simple Products

  1. Create a Product Type called YourInitials_Networking.
  2. Create three simple products under the Routers subcategory:
    • YourInitials_Router_Basic — Part Number: RTR100, Base Price: 149
    • YourInitials_Router_Pro — Part Number: RTR200, Base Price: 299
    • YourInitials_Router_Enterprise — Part Number: RTR300, Base Price: 599
  3. Set all to Display Type: Simple Product, Order Item Type: Sales, and restrict to your company.

Part C — Configurable Product

  1. Create a configurable product: YourInitials_Custom_Switch, Part Number: CSW001, Base Price: 499, Category: Switches.
  2. Create two attributes centrally:
    • YourInitials_Port_Count (User Selection): values 8 Ports, 16 Ports, 24 Ports, 48 Ports
    • YourInitials_Speed (User Selection): values 1Gbps, 10Gbps, 25Gbps
  3. Assign both attributes to the configurable product. Set Port Count as Required (Radio Button) and Speed as Required (Dropdown).
  4. Arrange the layout: Port Count first, then Speed.
  5. Test in Layout → Configuration Preview.

Validation

Browse the catalog as an end user. Verify the category hierarchy is correct, simple products are addable with one click, and the configurable product opens a configuration screen with both attributes. Confirm the product cannot be added until both required attributes are selected (status: Complete).

sections.title: Exercise 3 — Guided Selling Flow
sections.content:
Objective

Create a guided selling flow that helps a user find the right networking product based on selected criteria, using the same attributes defined for your configurable product.

Steps

  1. Navigate to Setup → Product Catalog → Guided Selling.
  2. Click Add New.
  3. On the Product Definition tab, set Name to YourInitials_Network_Finder and assign it to the YourInitials_Networking category. Set permissions to your company.
  4. On the Product Attributes/Options tab, add the attributes YourInitials_Port_Count and YourInitials_Speed.
  5. For each attribute, change the display type to Checkbox (so users can select multiple acceptable values).
  6. On the Configuration Layout tab, arrange the attributes in logical order: Port Count first, then Speed.
  7. Save.

Validation

Exit Setup and navigate to the catalog. Find the guided selling entry under the Networking category. Open it and select some criteria (e.g. 24 Ports + 10Gbps). Verify that the system returns matching products from the Networking category. Try different combinations and confirm the filtering works. Note how the interface looks identical to a configurable product screen — this is by design.

sections.title: Exercise 4 — Explore the Admin UI & Identify Key Areas
sections.content:
Objective

Systematically explore the CPQ admin interface and document what each major area contains. This builds navigation muscle memory that is essential for both daily work and the certification exam.

Steps

  1. Log into your CPQ sandbox as an administrator.
  2. Navigate through each major area and document what you find. For each area, write down: the menu path, what types of objects are managed there, and one example use case.

Areas to explore:

  • Setup → Product Catalog → Categories — note category/subcategory structure.
  • Setup → Product Catalog → Products — note the search filters (name, part number, display type, active status, system ID).
  • Setup → Product Catalog → Attributes — note how attributes are central and reusable.
  • Setup → Product Catalog → Guided Selling — note it's separate from products.
  • Setup → Product Catalog → Custom Tables — open an existing table (if any) and note the column structure and validity dates.
  • Setup → Users — note the Company → User Type → User → Permission Group data model.
  • Setup → Pricing/Calculations → Currencies — note the default currency and conversion rates.
  • Setup → Pricing/Calculations → Markets — note the market factor concept.
  • Setup → Pricing/Calculations → Pricebooks — select a market and explore any existing pricebooks.
  • Setup → Quote — explore Custom Quote Tabs, Custom Fields, Fields/Calculations/Layout, and Quote Tables.
  • Setup → Workflow/Approval — explore Statuses, Workflow Actions (table view), and Approval Rules.
  • Setup → Develop → Global Scripts — note where scripts are managed.
  • Setup → General → Application Parameters — note the key parameters (Enable Promotions, etc.).
  • Setup → Home — note the CPQ version number and release information.

Validation

Create a one-page cheat sheet mapping each admin area to its purpose. Compare it against the training material to ensure you haven't missed any key areas. This cheat sheet will be useful throughout your CPQ study.
